BreakEvenAnalysis.Context: Prepared ahead of December 2026 Building Committee meeting. Reviewing whether current pledge and offering income covers anticipated renovation costs, specifically the HVAC replacement and roof repair projects approved in 2025. Analysis based on Q1 2026 actuals.

CurrentState.Note: Monthly net income of $4,725 supports continued operations. Building Fund replenishment rate of ~$2,200/month means reserves can recover from either project within 6-8 months.

Scenario_A_RentTo750.Label: HVAC Replacement – Sanctuary (2026)
Scenario_A_RentTo750.BreakEvenCalculation: At $27.08/member, need 945 contributing members to break even after HVAC draw
Scenario_A_RentTo750.Impact: Project approved November 2026. Contractor: Gulf Coast HVAC Services. Work scheduled January 2027.

Scenario_B_RentTo850.Label: Roof Repair – Section B (2026)
Scenario_B_RentTo850.Impact: Pending contractor confirmation. If both HVAC and roof proceed in same fiscal year, combined draw of $31,250 leaves only $2,950 in building fund.

BreakEvenAnalysis.KeyInsight: Monthly net income of $4,725 supports continued operations. Building Fund replenishment rate of ~$2,200/month means reserves can recover from either project within 6-8 months. Dual-project execution in same year is not recommended without a targeted fundraising campaign.
ActionItems: Schedule HVAC replacement with Gulf Coast HVAC Services for January 2027 – Owner: Lamar Boykin, Due: 2026-12-20
Obtain second bid for roof repair from at least one additional contractor – Owner: Calvin Hargrove, Due: 2026-12-30
Present dual-project risk to congregation at June 15 business meeting – Owner: Deacon Marcus Pittman, Due: 2026-12-15
Update Building Fund pledge drive materials for summer push – Owner: Gloria Tate, Due: 2026-12-25
Review insurance coverage limits for HVAC and roofing work – Owner: Theresa Nguyen, Due: 2026-12-18
